insight: In the FIFA World Cup 2026, Czechia's performance during hydration breaks has been somewhat inconsistent, with the team managing to gain momentum in only half of the breaks they've had, resulting in a slight average decline in momentum of 0.150 minutes per break. When Czechia was pressing their opponents before a hydration break, they tended to lose momentum, with an average decline of 0.300 minutes per break, and were unable to regain it after the break. However, when they weren't pressing before a break, they were able to maintain their momentum, with no average change, which suggests that the team may benefit from a more conservative approach leading up to hydration breaks. Overall, these statistics suggest that Czechia's strategy and performance during hydration breaks could be an area for improvement in order to gain a competitive edge in the tournament.
